workflow.name: Summarize Japanese AI engineering articles from Qiita and note RSS to Slack
workflow.description:
## Who’s it for

This workflow is designed for Japanese-speaking individuals who want to efficiently stay up to date with practical, experience-based AI and engineering insights shared by developers on platforms like Qiita and note.

It specifically targets users who prefer real-world knowledge such as implementation examples, troubleshooting solutions, and hands-on AI use cases written in Japanese, rather than generalized global IT news or curated media content.

The workflow is optimized for those who want to quickly consume high-quality Japanese technical content on a daily basis.

---

## What it does

This workflow collects, processes, and summarizes Japanese AI and engineering-related articles published within the last 24 hours from Qiita and note RSS feeds.

It merges multiple RSS sources, filters only recent articles (last 24 hours), and prepares structured data for AI processing.

Then, it uses AI to evaluate and rank the articles, selects the most valuable ones, retrieves each article page, extracts readable content, and generates structured summaries in Japanese, including:

- Summary
- Target audience
- Use cases
- Merits
- Demerits

Finally, it formats the results and sends a daily digest to Slack in Japanese.

Users can also customize RSS sources to match their preferred content.

---

## How it works

1. A scheduled trigger starts the workflow automatically.
2. RSS feeds from Qiita and note are fetched and merged.
3. Articles are filtered to only include those published within the last 24 hours.
4. Articles are normalized into a structured format for AI processing.
5. Gemini evaluates and ranks articles based on usefulness and selects the top 10.
6. Article links are prepared and each page is fetched.
7. HTML is cleaned and converted into readable text.
8. OpenAI generates structured summaries in Japanese.
9. The final digest is formatted and posted to Slack in Japanese.

---

## Requirements

- Google Gemini API credentials
- OpenAI API credentials
- Slack OAuth2 credentials
- A Slack channel for notifications

---

## How to set up

Add your API credentials in n8n, set the Slack destination channel, review and adjust the AI prompts if needed, and activate the workflow.

You can also customize RSS sources depending on your preferred Japanese content (e.g., specific hashtags, niche blogs, or categories).

---

## How to customize the workflow

You can customize this workflow by:

- Adding or replacing RSS sources (e.g., Japanese niche engineering blogs or communities)
- Adjusting filtering conditions (e.g., time range beyond 24 hours or keyword-based filtering)
- Refining AI scoring criteria to better match your interests
- Modifying summary structure or output format (Japanese-focused customization)
- Customizing Slack message layout for better readability
- Changing the output language (default is Japanese)

workflow.name: 公認資格ワークフロー

parameters.text:
=あなたは初級エンジニア向けの記事評価AIです。

以下の記事一覧を評価してください。

【評価基準】

・実務で役立つか
・初心者でも理解できるか
・技術的価値があるか
・再現可能な具体的手順やコードが含まれているか
・明確な問題とその解決方法が提示されているか
・内容が具体的で抽象的すぎないか

【スコアルール】

・0〜100点の整数で評価すること
・50点が平均的な記事
・80点以上は非常に有益
・90点以上は実務で強く役立つ優良記事
・30点以下は価値が低い記事

【重要】

・QiitaおよびZennの記事の特性（実務的・実体験ベース）を考慮して評価すること
・抽象的なまとめ記事や紹介だけの記事は低評価にすること
・実装・検証・具体例がある記事を高評価にすること

・必ず指定されたJSON形式で返す
・配列で返す
・titleはそのまま使う
・scoreは必ず0〜100の整数で出す
・scoreが高い記事から10個の記事だけ表示する

【出力形式】

[
  {
    "title": "記事タイトル",
    "link": "URL",
    "score": 85,
    "reason": "理由"
  }
]

記事一覧:

{{ JSON.stringify($json.articles) }}

=あなたはデータ整形専用AIです。

以下の内容を分析し、必ずJSONのみを返してください。

【絶対ルール】
・JSON以外の文字を一切書かない
・説明文禁止
・前置き禁止
・コードブロック禁止（```禁止）
・改行OK
・必ず1つのJSONオブジェクトで返す
・keyは必ず以下と完全一致

【出力形式】
{
 "summary": "",
 "target": "",
 "use_case": "",
 "merit": "",
 "demerit": "",
 "link": ""
}

【追加ルール（重要）】

・merit（メリット）は200文字以上400文字以下で書くこと
・demerit（デメリット）も200文字以上400文字以下で書くこと

【入力】
リンク:
{{ $json.link }}

本文:
{{ $json.cleanText }}

parameters.content:
## STEP1: Data Collection
Fetch articles from Qiita and note RSS feeds and merge into one list

## STEP2: Data Filtering & Structuring
Filter articles within the last 24 hours and transform them into a structured format for AI processing

## STEP3: AI-Based Article Scoring and Selection
Evaluate each article using AI based on practical usefulness, readability for beginners, technical value, reproducibility, and problem-solving relevance.
The scored results are then split into individual articles, ranked, and filtered to select the top 10 actionable, experience-based articles.

## STEP4: Content Retrieval & Preparation
Prepare article links, fetch each article HTML, and extract and clean the main text content for further processing

## STEP5: AI Article Summarization
Generate summary, target, use cases, merits, and demerits

## STEP6: Notification
Format the result and send it to Slack

## AUTOMATE AI AND ENGINEERING ARTICLE SUMMARIZATION FROM RSS TO SLACK

## Who’s it for
Japanese engineers and learners who want to quickly stay updated with practical AI and engineering articles from Qiita and note.

## What it does
Fetches articles from RSS feeds, filters those published within the past 24 hours, ranks them with AI, summarizes key content, and sends a daily digest to Slack.

## How it works
1. Scheduled trigger runs the workflow
2. RSS feeds are fetched and merged
3. Articles from the past 24 hours are filtered and structured
4. AI ranks and selects top articles
5. Article content is retrieved and cleaned
6. AI generates summaries
7. Results are formatted and sent to Slack

## Requirements
- Google Gemini API credentials
- OpenAI API credentials
- Slack OAuth2 credentials
- A Slack channel for notifications

## How to set up
Configure API keys, set Slack channel, and activate the workflow.

## How to customize
- Change RSS sources
- Adjust filters or time range
- Modify AI prompts
- Customize Slack output
